fear: That the oxygen deprivation is already corrupting his hearing — that he is mishearing frequencies, misreading the cathode-ray trace, and that his confident analysis is built on a degrading instrument: his own brain.
name: Senior Petty Officer Dmitri Volkov
voice: Technical and involuntarily precise — he gives ranges in meters, bearings in degrees, and times in seconds, and becomes visibly uncomfortable when asked to summarize.
secrets: He heard what may have been a partial Moscow broadcast on the emergency frequency forty minutes ago — a carrier wave with fragments of encoded text — and logged it as interference because he could not confirm it. He has not told Arkhipov.
He is seventeen months into a three-year sentence of submarine service assigned as punishment for a political infraction at the Leningrad naval academy — he was not supposed to be on this boat.
knowledge: The acoustic signature of a Mk-84 signaling charge is distinct from a lethal depth charge — the former produces a sharp, high-frequency crack with rapid decay; the latter produces a low, rolling concussion with a pressure wave that arrives before the sound.
He has logged the exact bearing and estimated range of four surface ships — the USS Cony, USS Beale, USS Murray, and a fourth contact he cannot yet classify.
The most recent detonation was 47 meters off the port quarter — closer than the previous two, but still within the signaling envelope, not the kill envelope.
publicFace: A thin, intense young specialist who speaks in numbers and frequencies and seems more comfortable with the sounds of the ocean than with human conversation.
represents: Soviet hydroacoustic officers aboard Foxtrot-class submarines during the Cuban Missile Crisis, October 1962
trustLogic: Volkov trusts people who ask specific questions and distrust people who ask him to tell them what they want to hear — he responds to intellectual rigor and shuts down under emotional pressure.
privateGoal: To give Arkhipov the exact, correct acoustic analysis — not a guess, not a probability, but a certainty — because he understands that an imprecise answer in this compartment is the same as no answer.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He recites the data again, more slowly, as if the problem is that the listener did not understand the first time.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the possible Moscow broadcast until he can confirm it — and he cannot confirm it with the equipment available.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He stops, removes his headphones, and asks what specifically the questioner wants him to say — making clear that he will not falsify a reading.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He produces his manual tracing chart and reads from it verbatim, treating the document as his only defense.
